per_question.answer:
The products that consume Quantum Sketch's video API are offSchool and letX, as mentioned in notes ## bench-b9e4ccb4-dev-008 and ## bench-b9e4ccb4-dev-001, respectively. Additionally, note ## bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-007 mentions that three partner products depend on the API platform, but it does not specify the names of these products.

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

Context-Heavy switched its embedding provider to NVIDIA NIM because the OpenRouter key hit its total spending limit and started returning 403 errors [bench-b9e4ccb4-dev-005].

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

Context-Heavy currently uses the BGE-M3 embedding model at 1024 dimensions, as stated in notes **bench-b9e4ccb4-dev-006**.

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

Notes from common-knowledge get into Context-Heavy through the ck bridge, which pushes captured notes into Context-Heavy using POST /v1/sync/ck, allowing local notes to land in the cloud brain [bench-b9e4ccb4-dev-004, bench-b9e4ccb4-fnd-002].

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

The notes provided do not contain information about the price offSchool charges enterprise customers per seat. I used all the provided notes (bench-b9e4ccb4-dev-008, bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-005, bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-008, bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-010, bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-002, bench-b9e4ccb4-wr-001, bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-006, bench-b9e4ccb4-wr-004, bench-b9e4ccb4-wr-007, bench-b9e4ccb4-dev-005) to determine this.

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

Context-Heavy exposes a REST API and an MCP server over SSE so external agents can share its memory. I used note `bench-b9e4ccb4-dev-003` for this information.

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

Yes, Context-Heavy originally ran without an ANN index on its embeddings. According to note **bench-b9e4ccb4-dev-007**, Context-Heavy's embeddings originally used a free multimodal model with an unconstrained vector column and no ANN index. 

**What the brain doesn't know:** Nothing notable.

Context-Heavy is not deployed yet because its retrieval engine has not beaten competitor benchmarks. According to note **bench-b9e4ccb4-fnd-003**, the decision was made to keep Context-Heavy undeployed until it can achieve better performance, as shipping with weak numbers would hurt its positioning more than shipping late.

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

The main benchmark target competitor is GBrain, which claims a +31 precision-at-5 lift coming from its knowledge graph. (Used note: bench-b9e4ccb4-fnd-006)

**What the brain doesn't know:** Nothing notable.

Context-Heavy and common-knowledge differ in their deployment models as Context-Heavy is cloud-first and private, while common-knowledge is local-first and open source. They share data through the ck bridge but have opposite deployment models [bench-b9e4ccb4-fnd-002, bench-b9e4ccb4-dev-004].

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

The provided notes do not contain information about the amount of funding the company has raised so far. I used all the provided notes (bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-004, bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-007, bench-b9e4ccb4-wr-004, bench-b9e4ccb4-fnd-001, bench-b9e4ccb4-fnd-005, bench-b9e4ccb4-dev-002, bench-b9e4ccb4-fnd-006, bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-003, bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-005, bench-b9e4ccb4-dev-008) to determine this.

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

The target audience for Context-Heavy is anyone, including normal users, developers, founders, and working professionals, as well as any AI agent they use [bench-b9e4ccb4-fnd-005].

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

The core product thesis around efficiency is that every benchmark score is weighted by token cost and latency, not accuracy alone, with the goal of creating the most efficient brain, not just the most accurate. (bench-b9e4ccb4-fnd-007)

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

According to note bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-001, a user who just signs in but does nothing does not count toward the engagement number, as an active user is defined as someone who completes at least one core action in a rolling 7-day window, not just a login.

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

We are currently measuring success by the Q2 north-star metric, which is weekly active teams, as stated in notes **bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-003** and **bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-004**. Before, we were measuring success by the Q1 north-star metric, which was total signups, also mentioned in **bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-003** and **bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-004**.

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

We shortened the signup flow because funnel data showed a 60% drop-off on step four, as stated in note **bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-002**. This decision was made to improve the onboarding process by cutting it from five steps to three.

**What the brain doesn't know:** Nothing notable.

The native app was delayed to focus all engineering on the API platform, which three partner products depend on (bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-007). The API platform received the engineering time instead.

**What the brain doesn't know:** Nothing notable.

We owe a free-tier customer a first reply within 24 hours. (bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-008)

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

The compliance certification blocking enterprise deals is SOC 2 Type II, which is scheduled for audit in Q3. (bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-005)

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

We step in at a churn level of logo churn above 3% in any month [bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-009].

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

The notes provided do not contain information about the headcount plan for the design team next quarter. 

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

The reason to build a custom benchmark instead of only using LongMemEval and LoCoMo is that existing benchmarks miss real cross-project recall and causal 'why did we decide' recall that professionals actually rely on (bench-b9e4ccb4-res-003).

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

Context-Heavy fuses full-text and vector search results using Reciprocal Rank Fusion with the constant k=60, as stated in note **bench-b9e4ccb4-res-004**.

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

The reranker model that reorders candidates after fusion is the cross-encoder reranker (nvidia/rerank-qa-mistral-4b) ([bench-b9e4ccb4-res-005](http://bench-b9e4ccb4-res-005)).

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

The provided notes do not contain Context-Heavy's exact F1 score on the SQuAD benchmark. I used all the provided notes to search for this information, but it was not found in any of them, including bench-b9e4ccb4-res-004, bench-b9e4ccb4-dev-007, bench-b9e4ccb4-fnd-003, bench-b9e4ccb4-dev-006, bench-b9e4ccb4-dev-003, bench-b9e4ccb4-dev-010, bench-b9e4ccb4-fnd-005, bench-b9e4ccb4-dev-005, bench-b9e4ccb4-fnd-002, and bench-b9e4ccb4-dev-008.

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

Context-Heavy's chunking parameters are described in note bench-b9e4ccb4-res-006. According to this note, chunking splits documents into 1200-byte chunks with 200 bytes of overlap, preferring paragraph then sentence boundaries. Each node keeps its two best-matching chunks via max-pooling.

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

The metrics that need an LLM-as-judge rather than ranked-list math are answer quality metrics, specifically faithfulness and abstention correctness, as stated in note **bench-b9e4ccb4-res-007**.

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

You may use a serial comma (also known as the Oxford comma) in legal disclaimers, as stated in note bench-b9e4ccb4-wr-001. However, it is forbidden in body copy.

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

Since your piece is approximately 3,000 words, which exceeds the 2,400-word cap for long-form pieces, it will be split into a series (bench-b9e4ccb4-wr-002). The blog publishes on Thursday (bench-b9e4ccb4-wr-003), so part one would go live on a Thursday. 

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

We stopped writing about the company as 'they' and started writing as 'I' because readers engaged 40% more with the founder's personal essays, as stated in note **bench-b9e4ccb4-wr-004**.

**What the brain doesn't know:** Nothing notable.

According to note `bench-b9e4ccb4-wr-005`, every claim with a statistic must link a primary source, and secondary aggregators are not acceptable citations. Since Statista is a secondary aggregator, it cannot be used to back a figure. 

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

To answer your question, I used notes **bench-b9e4ccb4-wr-006**. According to this note, if you write a new cornerstone article (referred to as a "pillar page"), it should be supported by six cluster posts that internally link up to it. These posts are expected to be published over the course of a quarter, as the SEO target is one pillar page per quarter.

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

According to note `bench-b9e4ccb4-wr-010`, since the two-year-old tutorial still pulls about 800 visits a month, which is above the 500 visits/month threshold, it should be refreshed rather than retired.

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

The rights to a piece written by an outside contributor are retained by the contributor, but they grant a perpetual, non-exclusive license. The paperwork, specifically the contract template, can be found in the legal folder ([bench-b9e4ccb4-wr-007](#)).

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.

The notes provided do not contain information about the retainer rate for freelance editors. 

I used all the provided notes to search for this information, but none of them (bench-b9e4ccb4-wr-007, bench-b9e4ccb4-wr-002, bench-b9e4ccb4-wr-004, bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-008, bench-b9e4ccb4-wr-010, bench-b9e4ccb4-wr-003, bench-b9e4ccb4-wr-009, bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-009, bench-b9e4ccb4-pm-002, bench-b9e4ccb4-res-007) mention the retainer rate for freelance editors.

**What the brain doesn't know:** 
Nothing notable.
